sentences.en: The environmental practices of big businesses are shaped by a fundamental fact that for many of us offends our sense of justice.
sentences.zh: 大企业的环境行为是由一个基本事实所塑造的，而这个事实在我们许多人看来有违正义感。

grammar.type: 同位语从句 + 定语从句
grammar.note: a fundamental fact that... 中 that 引导同位语从句说明 fact 的内容；that 从句内 for many of us offends our sense of justice 又含定语从句性质，实为 “which offends...” 修饰 fact；be shaped by 意为 “由……塑造”。

sentences.en: Depending on the circumstances, a business may maximize the amount of money it makes, at least in the short term, by damaging the environment and hurting people.
sentences.zh: 视具体情况而定，企业可能通过破坏环境和损害他人来实现其收益最大化——至少在短期内如此。

grammar.type: 现在分词状语 + by 方式状语
grammar.note: Depending on the circumstances 为现在分词短语作条件状语；by damaging... and hurting... 为 by + 动名词表方式；at least in the short term 为插入的让步性状语；the amount of money (that) it makes 含省略 that 的定语从句。

sentences.en: That is still the case today for fishermen in an unmanaged fishery without quotas, and for international logging companies with short-term leases on tropical rainforest land in places with corrupt officials and unsophisticated landowners.
sentences.zh: 如今对于在无管理、无配额渔场作业的渔民，以及在官员腐败、地主缺乏经验的地区持有热带雨林土地短期租约的国际伐木公司来说，情况依然如此。

grammar.type: 并列介词短语 + 后置定语
grammar.note: for fishermen... and for... logging companies... 为两个并列的 for 短语；in an unmanaged fishery without quotas 及 with short-term leases on... land 均为后置定语；in places with corrupt officials and unsophisticated landowners 进一步限定地点；That is the case 意为 “情况就是这样”。

words.def: 不谙世故的；缺乏经验的

sentences.en: When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn’t care.
sentences.zh: 当政府监管有效、公众具有环保意识时，环保清洁型大企业可能会在竞争中胜过污染型企业；但如果政府监管无力、公众漠不关心，情况则可能恰恰相反。

grammar.type: when 时间状语从句 + but 转折 + if 条件从句
grammar.note: 两个 when 从句并列作条件；主句 environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones；but 后 the reverse is likely to be true 接两个 if 条件从句；out-compete 意为 “在竞争中胜过”；the reverse 意为 “相反的情况”。

sentences.en: It is easy for the rest of us to blame a business for helping itself by hurting other people.
sentences.zh: 对我们其他人来说，指责一家企业为了自身利益而损害他人是很容易的。

grammar.type: it 形式主语 + blame sb for doing
grammar.note: It is easy for the rest of us to blame... 中 it 为形式主语，真正主语为 to blame 不定式；blame sb for (doing) sth 意为 “因某事责备某人”；by hurting other people 为 by + 动名词表方式。

sentences.en: But blaming alone is unlikely to produce change.
sentences.zh: 但仅仅指责不太可能带来改变。

grammar.type: 动名词作主语
grammar.note: blaming alone 为动名词短语作主语，alone 修饰 blaming 表 “仅仅、单单”；be unlikely to do 意为 “不太可能做”。

sentences.en: It ignores the fact that businesses are not charities but profit-making companies, and that publicly owned companies with shareholders are under obligation to those shareholders to maximize profits, provided that they do so by legal means.
sentences.zh: 它忽视了这样一个事实：企业不是慈善机构，而是营利性公司；并且拥有股东的上市公司有义务为股东实现利润最大化——只要它们以合法方式做到这一点。

grammar.type: 两个 that 同位语从句并列 + provided that 条件
grammar.note: the fact that... and that... 为两个并列的同位语从句说明 fact；not... but... 意为 “不是……而是……”；be under obligation to sb to do 意为 “有义务为某人做”；provided that 意为 “只要、假如”引导条件从句。

sentences.en: US laws make a company’s directors legally liable for something termed ‘breach of fiduciary responsibility’ if they knowingly manage a company in a way that reduces profits.
sentences.zh: 美国法律规定，如果公司董事明知会减少利润却仍以某种方式经营公司，他们将对被称为“违反受托责任”的行为承担法律责任。

grammar.type: make sb liable + 过去分词定语 + if 条件从句
grammar.note: make + 宾语 + liable 为 “使……负有责任”；termed 'breach of fiduciary responsibility' 为过去分词短语作定语修饰 something；if 从句表条件；in a way that reduces profits 含定语从句修饰 way。

sentences.en: The car manufacturer Henry Ford was in fact successfully sued by shareholders in 1919 for raising the minimum wage of his workers to $5 per day: the courts declared that, while Ford’s humanitarian sentiments about his employees were nice, his business existed to make profits for its stockholders.
sentences.zh: 事实上，汽车制造商亨利·福特曾在1919年因将工人的最低日薪提高到5美元而被股东成功起诉：法院裁定，尽管福特对雇员的人道主义情感很好，但他的企业存在的目的是为股东创造利润。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 冒号解释 + while 让步从句
grammar.note: was sued by... for doing 为被动语态，for 表原因；冒号后为法院裁定内容；while Ford's humanitarian sentiments... were nice 为 while 引导的让步状语从句；his business existed to make profits 为主句。

sentences.en: Our blaming of businesses also ignores the ultimate responsibility of the public for creating the conditions that let a business profit through destructive environmental policies.
sentences.zh: 我们对企业的指责还忽视了公众负有的终极责任——正是公众创造了让企业通过破坏性环境政策获利的条件。

grammar.type: for 介词短语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: the ultimate responsibility of the public for creating... 中 for creating 说明责任的内容；the conditions that let a business profit... 含定语从句，that 指代 conditions 作主语，let sb do 为使役结构；through destructive environmental policies 表方式。

sentences.en: In the long run, it is the public, either directly or through its politicians, that has the power to make such destructive policies unprofitable and illegal, and to make sustainable environmental policies profitable.
sentences.zh: 从长远来看，正是公众——无论是直接地还是通过其政治代表——拥有使这类破坏性政策无利可图且违法、并使可持续环境政策有利可图的力量。

grammar.type: it is ... that 强调句
grammar.note: it is the public... that has the power 为强调句，强调主语 the public；either directly or through its politicians 为插入的方式状语；两个 to make... 不定式并列作 power 的后置定语；make sth unprofitable/illegal/profitable 为 “使……成为……”。

sentences.en: The public can do that by suing businesses for harming them, as happened after the Exxon Valdez disaster, in which over 40,000 m3 of oil were spilled off the coast of Alaska.
sentences.zh: 公众可以通过起诉那些损害他们利益的企业来做到这一点，正如埃克森·瓦尔迪兹号灾难之后所发生的那样——在那次事故中，超过4万立方米的石油泄漏到阿拉斯加海岸外。

grammar.type: by 方式状语 + as 非限制性 + in which 定语从句
grammar.note: by suing businesses for harming them 为 by + 动名词表方式；as happened after... 为 as 引导的非限制性定语从句，as 指代前面整句内容；in which... 为定语从句修饰 disaster；were spilled 为被动语态。

sentences.en: The public may also make their opinion felt by preferring to buy sustainably harvested products; by making employees of companies with poor track records feel ashamed of their company and complain to their own management; by preferring their governments to award valuable contracts to businesses with a good environmental track record; and by pressing their governments to pass and enforce laws and regulations requiring good environmental practices.
sentences.zh: 公众还可以通过以下方式表达自己的意见：优先购买以可持续方式采集的产品；使那些业绩记录不佳的公司的员工为自己的公司感到羞愧并向本公司管理层投诉；倾向于让政府把有价值的合同授予具有良好环境记录的企业；以及敦促政府制定并执行要求良好环境行为的法律法规。

grammar.type: make sth felt + 四个 by 短语并列
grammar.note: make their opinion felt 为 “使意见被感知/表达出来”；分号隔开四个并列的 by + 动名词短语；make employees... feel ashamed... and complain 为使役结构带两并列动词；requiring good environmental practices 为现在分词作定语修饰 laws and regulations。

words.def: 表达/让人感受到自己的意见

sentences.en: In turn, big businesses can exert powerful pressure on any suppliers that might ignore public or government pressure.
sentences.zh: 反过来，大企业也可以对任何可能无视公众或政府压力的供应商施加强大压力。

grammar.type: 定语从句
grammar.note: that might ignore public or government pressure 为定语从句修饰 suppliers；In turn 意为 “反过来、依次”；exert pressure on 意为 “对……施加压力”。

sentences.en: For instance, after the US public became concerned about the spread of a disease known as BSE, which was transmitted to humans through infected meat, the US government’s Food and Drug Administration introduced rules demanding that the meat industry abandon practices associated with the risk of the disease spreading.
sentences.zh: 例如，在美国公众开始担忧一种名为“疯牛病”（BSE）的疾病传播之后——这种疾病通过受感染的肉类传染给人类——美国政府的食品药品管理局出台规定，要求肉类行业放弃那些与疾病传播风险相关的做法。

grammar.type: after 时间从句 + which 非限定 + demanding 分词定语(虚拟)
grammar.note: after... became concerned about 为时间状语从句；which was transmitted... 为非限制性定语从句修饰 BSE/disease；demanding that the meat industry abandon... 为现在分词作定语，demand 后从句用虚拟语气(should) abandon 省略 should；associated with... 为过去分词定语修饰 practices。

sentences.en: But for five years the meat packers refused to follow these, claiming that they would be too expensive to obey.
sentences.zh: 但在长达五年的时间里，肉类加工商拒绝遵守这些规定，声称遵守它们成本太高。

grammar.type: 现在分词状语 + too...to...
grammar.note: claiming that... 为现在分词短语作伴随状语；they would be too expensive to obey 为 too...to... 结构，意为 “太贵而无法遵守”；refuse to do 意为 “拒绝做”。

sentences.en: However, when a major fast-food company then made the same demands after customer purchases of its hamburgers plummeted, the meat industry complied within weeks.
sentences.zh: 然而，当一家大型快餐公司在其汉堡销量骤降之后提出同样的要求时，肉类行业在几周之内就照办了。

grammar.type: when 时间从句 + after 时间从句嵌套
grammar.note: when a major fast-food company then made the same demands 为时间状语从句，其中又嵌套 after customer purchases... plummeted 时间从句；主句 the meat industry complied within weeks；comply 意为 “遵从、照办”。

sentences.en: The public’s task is therefore to identify which links in the supply chain are sensitive to public pressure: for instance, fast-food chains or jewelry stores, but not meat packers or gold miners.
sentences.zh: 因此，公众的任务是找出供应链中哪些环节对公众压力敏感：例如，快餐连锁店或珠宝店（对压力敏感），而肉类加工商或金矿开采者则不然。

grammar.type: which 引导宾语从句 + 冒号举例
grammar.note: identify which links... are sensitive to 中 which links... 为 identify 的宾语从句；冒号后为举例说明；but not... 表对比，省略了重复的谓语；be sensitive to 意为 “对……敏感”。

sentences.en: Some readers may be disappointed or outraged that I place the ultimate responsibility for business practices harming the public on the public itself.
sentences.zh: 一些读者可能会感到失望或愤怒，因为我把损害公众的商业行为的终极责任归咎于公众自身。

grammar.type: 形容词后 that 从句 + place ... on ...
grammar.note: disappointed or outraged that... 中 that 从句说明情绪的原因；place the responsibility for... on the public itself 为 place A on B 结构，意为 “把 A 归于 B”；harming the public 为现在分词作定语修饰 business practices。

sentences.en: I also believe that the public must accept the necessity for higher prices for products to cover the added costs, if any, of sound environmental practices.
sentences.zh: 我还认为，公众必须接受产品涨价的必要性，以弥补良好环境行为所增加的成本（如果有的话）。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + 不定式目的状语
grammar.note: believe that... 为宾语从句；the necessity for higher prices... to cover the added costs 中 to cover 表目的；if any 为插入语意为 “如果有的话”；of sound environmental practices 为 costs 的后置定语；sound 意为 “良好的、合理的”。

sentences.en: My views may seem to ignore the belief that businesses should act in accordance with moral principles even if this leads to a reduction in their profits.
sentences.zh: 我的观点看似忽视了这样一种信念：企业应当依照道德原则行事，即便这会导致其利润减少。

grammar.type: 同位语从句 + even if 让步从句
grammar.note: the belief that businesses should act... 中 that 引导同位语从句说明 belief；even if this leads to... 为 even if 让步状语从句；in accordance with 意为 “依照、与……一致”；seem to do 意为 “似乎做”。

sentences.en: But I think we have to recognize that, throughout human history, in all politically complex human societies, government regulation has arisen precisely because it was found that not only did moral principles need to be made explicit, they also needed to be enforced.
sentences.zh: 但我认为我们必须认识到：纵观人类历史，在所有政治结构复杂的人类社会中，政府监管的出现恰恰是因为人们发现，道德原则不仅需要被明确化，还需要被强制执行。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + because 原因 + not only 倒装
grammar.note: recognize that... 为宾语从句；because it was found that... 表原因；that 从句内 not only did moral principles need... 因 not only 置于句首而部分倒装；they also needed to be enforced 为并列分句；be made explicit 意为 “被明确化”。

sentences.en: To me, the conclusion that the public has the ultimate responsibility for the behavior of even the biggest businesses is empowering and hopeful, rather than disappointing.
sentences.zh: 对我而言，“公众对哪怕是最大企业的行为负有终极责任”这一结论令人振奋、充满希望，而非令人失望。

grammar.type: 同位语从句 + rather than
grammar.note: the conclusion that the public has... 中 that 引导同位语从句说明 conclusion；主句谓语 is empowering and hopeful；rather than disappointing 表对比，意为 “而不是令人失望”；even 修饰 the biggest businesses 表强调。

words.def: 赋予力量的；令人振奋的

sentences.en: My conclusion is not a moralistic one about who is right or wrong, admirable or selfish, a good guy or a bad guy.
sentences.zh: 我的结论并非关于谁对谁错、谁值得敬佩谁自私自利、谁是好人谁是坏人的道德评判。

grammar.type: about 后并列宾语从句/名词短语
grammar.note: not a moralistic one 中 one 指代 conclusion；about who is right or wrong... 为介词 about 后的宾语从句；三组反义并列 right or wrong / admirable or selfish / a good guy or a bad guy 作表语。

words.def: 道德说教的；道德评判的

sentences.en: In the past, businesses have changed when the public came to expect and require different behavior, to reward businesses for behavior that the public wanted, and to make things difficult for businesses practicing behaviors that the public didn’t want.
sentences.zh: 在过去，当公众开始期待并要求不同的行为、开始奖励做出公众所希望行为的企业、并给做出公众所不希望行为的企业制造困难时，企业就发生了改变。

grammar.type: when 时间从句 + 三个不定式并列
grammar.note: when the public came to expect and require... 为时间状语从句；to reward... 和 to make things difficult... 与 (came) to expect 并列，均为 came to do 结构；两处 that the public wanted / didn't want 为定语从句；practicing behaviors... 为现在分词定语修饰 businesses。

sentences.en: I predict that in the future, just as in the past, changes in public attitudes will be essential for changes in businesses’ environmental practices.
sentences.zh: 我预测，未来正如过去一样，公众态度的改变将是企业环境行为发生改变的必要条件。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + just as 类比状语
grammar.note: predict that... 为宾语从句；just as in the past 为 just as 引导的方式/类比状语，插入主句之中；be essential for 意为 “对……至关重要/是……的必要条件”；businesses' 为名词所有格。

questions.title: Questions 27–31
instructions: Complete the summary using the list of words, A–J, below.
Write the correct letter, A–J, in boxes 27–31 on your answer sheet.
Word list: A funding  B trees  C rare species  D moral standards  E control  F involvement  G flooding  H overfishing  I worker support

items.prompt: Many big businesses today are prepared to harm people and the environment in order to make money, and they appear to have no 27 ______.
Lack of 28 ______ by governments ... can lead to environmental problems.
... and lack of public 29 ______ can lead to environmental problems.

questions.title: Questions 32–34
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 32–34 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt:
The main idea of the third paragraph is that environmental damage
A requires political action if it is to be stopped.
B is the result of ignorance on the part of the public.
C could be prevented by the action of ordinary people.
D can only be stopped by educating business leaders.

In the fourth paragraph, the writer describes ways in which the public can
A reduce their own individual impact on the environment.
B learn more about the impact of business on the environment.
C raise awareness of the effects of specific environmental disasters.
D influence the environmental policies of businesses and governments.

What pressure was exerted by big business in the case of the disease BSE?
A Meat packers stopped supplying hamburgers to fast-food chains.
B A fast-food company forced their meat suppliers to follow the law.
C Meat packers persuaded the government to reduce their expenses.
D A fast-food company encouraged the government to introduce legislation.

questions.title: Questions 35–39
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 35–39 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: The public should be prepared to fund good environmental practices.
There is a contrast between the moral principles of different businesses.
It is important to make a clear distinction between acceptable and unacceptable behaviour.
The public have successfully influenced businesses in the past.
In the future, businesses will show more concern for the environment.

questions.title: Question 40
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in box 40 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt:
What would be the best subheading for this passage?
A Will the world survive the threat caused by big businesses?
B How can big businesses be encouraged to be less driven by profit?
C What environmental dangers are caused by the greed of businesses?
D Are big businesses to blame for the damage they cause the environment?
